classObjective: Graph piecewise-defined functions and step functions (greatest integer functions) and identify their domain and range.
workedExamples: 4-6, Example 2 — Graph Step Functions (Greatest Integer Function)
warmUp: What is floor(3.7)? What is floor(-2.3)?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: closed and open circle notation; floor function notation; step structure; domain/range identification for piecewise functions
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice graphing piecewise-defined and step functions with correct notation
independentPractice: Practice problems graphing piecewise and step functions and identifying their domain and range
assessment: Exit ticket: graph the piecewise function: f(x) = {2x if x < 0, x + 3 if x ≥ 0} and state its domain and range
capReflection: Why do step functions have different notation conventions than continuous piecewise functions?
